title: Automated Decision-Making and Profiling (ADMP)
description: Regulatory regime for automated decision-making and profiling (including AI / Generative AI use), data subject rights, exceptions, and special protections for biometric data and CCTV. Aligned with the JPDP ADMP Guideline issued 30 April 2026 (PCP No. 3/2025 substantive content).

guidelineStatus.issuingAuthority: Personal Data Protection Commissioner Shariffah Rashidah Syed Othman
guidelineStatus.verificationNote: Module reflects the March 2025 consultation paper. Verify against issued PDF when available at https://www.pdp.gov.my/ppdpv1/akta/garis-panduan-ms/.

legalBasis.description: Act 709 does not yet specifically address automated decision-making and profiling. The ADMP Guideline introduces the regulatory regime under the Commissioner's authority. Cross-references s4 (definition of biometric data, included in sensitive personal data per A1727), s5 (mandatory PDP Principles compliance), s6 (consent), s17 (sensitive personal data conditions), s45 (general exemptions, replicated for ADMP exemptions).

structure.definitions: automated-decisions/definitions.json — automated decision-making, profiling, biometric data
structure.triggers: automated-decisions/triggers.json — when ADMP regulation applies (legal effects / significantly affects)
structure.rights: automated-decisions/data-subject-rights.json — Right to Refuse, Right to Information, Right to Human Review
structure.ai: automated-decisions/ai-generative-ai.json — AI/Generative AI as ADM tool
structure.cctv: automated-decisions/cctv.json — CCTV use for biometric processing

coreObligations: Identify which processing operations constitute automated decision-making or profiling
Assess whether each operation legally affects or significantly affects data subjects (the regulatory trigger)
Where triggered: implement Right to Refuse, Right to Information, and Right to Human Review
Where Exceptions apply: still maintain Right to Information (transparency) — this right is not subject to Exceptions
For sensitive personal data: only process under explicit consent or public interest, with safeguards
Children: do not profile or apply automated decision-making for advertising/marketing unless demonstrably in their best interests
Biometric data: special storage, security, accuracy, transparency, and disposal measures
AI / Generative AI deemed an ADM tool — additional safeguards (dignity, accuracy, no manipulation, human oversight, no pure-AI policy decisions)

internationalAlignment.gdpr: Article 22 (right not to be subject to a decision based solely on automated processing); Article 4(4) (definition of profiling)
internationalAlignment.indonesia: Personal Data Protection Law profiling provisions
internationalAlignment.china: Personal Information Protection Law (PIPL) automated decision-making provisions
internationalAlignment.euAiAct: Risk-based AI regulation overlapping where AI systems make decisions affecting individuals
internationalAlignment.euDsa: Digital Services Act — bans profiling-based ads for special category data; bans targeted ads to minors

crossRepoLinks.AI-Governance: Cross-link AI/Gen-AI controls — ADMP triggers AI accountability obligations
crossRepoLinks.dpia: DPIA quantitative threshold of >10,000 subjects for ADM means most regulated ADMP also requires DPIA
crossRepoLinks.dpbd: DPbD principles for sensitive data and children's processing apply to ADMP design
